edits.slides.6.content: I built this in code, not Figma. A static screen can't show whether toolbar placement breaks scanning rhythm when a clinician reaches for a tool.
edits.slides.6.bullets2.1: Design changes tested same-day without a dev cycle
edits.slides.6.highlight: Building in code was the only way to catch timing failures a static screen can't surface.
edits.slides.21.whatWorked.0: The coded prototype caught timing and rhythm failures Figma never would have.
edits.slides.21.whatWorked.1: Mapping the full flow first — every goal had a handoff failure to answer.
edits.slides.21.whatFailed.0: Toolbar testing came too late — work that looked done had to be revisited.
edits.slides.21.whatFailed.1: The icon redesign got bundled into toolbar work — it deserved its own phase.
edits.slides.21.whatYoudDoDifferently.0: Share the journey map with the team in week one — not after my own conclusions.
edits.slides.21.whatYouCouldntMeasure: [ADD: post-launch rescan rate and setup time delta — prototype testing validated the design; production data not yet available]
